ideas.title: AI Partner Health & Risk Monitor
ideas.description:
Problem Statement:
Partner Managers currently need to manually monitor partner performance across multiple dashboards and reports. Declining partner activity, abnormal traffic patterns, or early signs of churn may go unnoticed until significant business impact has already occurred.

Proposed Solution:
Develop an AI-powered monitoring system that continuously analyzes partner activity and automatically identifies risks, opportunities, and anomalies.

The AI would monitor:

Registrations
Deposits
Trading volume
Conversion rates
Campaign engagement
Traffic quality

The system would proactively generate alerts such as:

Partner activity is declining by 30%
Unusual registration spikes detected
High-potential partners showing growth trends
Recommended actions to improve performance
ideas.expectedBenefits:
Expected Benefits:

Early detection of partner churn risks
Faster intervention by Partner Managers
Improved partner retention
Better allocation of account management resources

Business Impact:

Increased partner retention
Improved revenue protection
Reduced manual monitoring effort
Better partner relationship management

Estimated ROI:
Very High

reviews.analysis: Excellent efficiency gains. Saving marketing managers several hours per campaign translates directly to direct cost reduction and faster time-to-market.
High feasibility. Generative templates for text are straightforward with established LLM libraries. APIs exist for standard social channels.
Significantly improves productivity for the marketing and growth teams, making it easier to orchestrate webinars.
Highly original application tailored perfectly to Deriv's frequent webinar campaigns.
Fully meets corporate compliance requirements. Standard token storage and secure channel communications prevent unauthorized access during campaign orchestration.
Empowers smoother user communication flow. Generating timely webinar invites and summaries directly enhances client experience and retention.
Very strong business impact by protecting the firm from catastrophic compliance breaches or regulatory fines.
Moderate complexity but very doable with structured external data. Easy integration with internal audit tooling.
Improves compliance audit workflow. Cuts background research times from hours to seconds.
Transformational regulatory risk modeling using agentic news screening.
Directly reinforces security. Safeguards partner evaluation processes and runs real-time behavior pattern scans safely within secure networks.
Builds platform trust and integrity. Demonstrating stringent partner vetting directly improves customer confidence and long-term brand equity.
Strong potential to improve customer retention. Targeted offers can lead to higher lifetime value and conversion rates.
Highly feasible. Customer segmentation models are well-understood and can be built using standard machine learning libraries and historical CRM data.
Reduces manual marketing campaign planning. Allows marketers to easily target segments with minimal overhead.
Good application of predictive modeling to CRM data, though similar systems are standard in mature marketing suites.
Complies with customer privacy and data localization frameworks. Strictly anonymizes client CRM profiles before predictive clustering passes.
Provides highly relevant, custom experiences for platform users, avoiding generic marketing spam and maximizing adoption rates.

Protecting partner-driven revenue streams directly impacts our top-line stability, making the ROI on this tool exceptionally high. By automating anomaly detection, we mitigate the financial risk of sudden partner churn before it impacts quarterly earnings.

While the machine learning models for anomaly detection are standard, the primary challenge lies in consolidating disparate real-time data streams into a unified pipeline. We must ensure data latency is minimized to make the proactive alerts truly actionable.

This tool will significantly reduce cognitive fatigue for Partner Managers by eliminating the need to manually scan multiple dashboards daily. It shifts their role from reactive firefighting to proactive relationship management, boosting morale and productivity.

While predictive analytics and anomaly detection are mature AI patterns, applying them specifically to partner lifecycle management provides a solid operational edge. It is a highly practical application of AI rather than a groundbreaking technological leap.

Monitoring partner trading volumes and deposits introduces compliance risks regarding data privacy and financial regulations. We will need strict role-based access controls and data masking to ensure this monitoring system does not expose sensitive partner information.

By identifying struggling partners early, our team can intervene with targeted support, dramatically improving the partner experience and retention rates. High-performing partners will also benefit from timely recognition and growth recommendations, strengthening our ecosystem.

summaries.summary:
### Executive Summary: AI Partner Health & Risk Monitor

**Problem Statement:** 
Partner Managers currently rely on manual, fragmented dashboard monitoring to track partner performance, leaving the organization vulnerable to undetected partner churn, abnormal traffic anomalies, and delayed interventions that directly threaten revenue stability.

**Proposed Solution:** 
Implement an AI-powered monitoring engine that ingests real-time multi-channel data streams—including registrations, deposits, trading volumes, and traffic quality—to run predictive anomaly detection models that automatically trigger proactive risk alerts and prescriptive action recommendations.

**Expected Benefits:** 
This system delivers a very high ROI by safeguarding partner-driven revenue streams, reducing manual monitoring overhead, and preventing churn. It mitigates cognitive fatigue for Partner Managers, shifting their workflow from reactive firefighting to proactive, high-value relationship management and targeted partner support.

**Implementation Recommendation:** 
Launch a phased pilot prioritizing the integration of disparate data sources into a unified, low-latency data pipeline. To address compliance and security requirements, the architecture must incorporate strict role-based access controls (RBAC) and data masking for sensitive financial and volume metrics.

### Executive Summary: Smart CRM Segmentation

**Problem Statement:** Traditional static CRM segmentation relies on manual campaign planning, leading to high operational overhead and generic marketing outreach. This lack of personalization results in suboptimal engagement and accelerated customer churn among dormant users.

**Proposed Solution:** We propose deploying predictive machine learning models to dynamically cluster clients based on behavioral data, utilizing strictly anonymized CRM profiles to trigger automated, highly targeted offers. This architecture integrates standard ML libraries with existing data pipelines to automate campaign targeting while ensuring strict data privacy compliance.

**Expected Benefits:** 
* **Performance Lift:** A projected 25% increase in email open-rates and a measurable lift in dormant user retention and lifetime value (LTV).
* **Operational Efficiency:** Significant reduction in manual marketing planning overhead through automated segment generation.
* **Security & UX:** Enhanced customer experience via hyper-relevant, non-spammy offers, secured by robust data localization and anonymization frameworks.

**Implementation Recommendation:** Launch a 6-week pilot utilizing historical CRM data to train and validate the predictive clustering models. Target a high-value dormant user cohort first to measure conversion lift and refine the algorithm before full-scale integration.

prds.markdown:
# Product Requirements Document (PRD): AI Webinar Campaign Planner

## 1. Executive Overview & Goals

### 1.1 Executive Summary
The **AI Webinar Campaign Planner** is an enterprise-grade generative AI platform designed to automate the end-to-end orchestration of webinar marketing campaigns. Currently, marketing and growth teams spend significant manual effort (averaging 12–15 hours per campaign) drafting landing page copy, structuring multi-stage email sequences, writing social media promotional posts, and manually scheduling these assets across disparate channels. 

This product automates these workflows by leveraging advanced Large Language Models (LLMs) and deep API integrations with marketing automation and social media platforms. By enabling a "1-click campaign launch," the platform reduces manual marketing orchestration overhead by 70%, accelerates time-to-market, and ensures brand-aligned, high-converting communication flows that directly enhance client acquisition and retention.

### 1.2 Business Objectives
*   **Efficiency & Cost Reduction:** Reduce the average time spent planning and launching a webinar campaign from 12 hours to under 15 minutes (a >95% reduction in active manual labor, exceeding the target 70% overhead reduction).
*   **Time-to-Market:** Enable growth teams to respond to market trends instantly by launching comprehensive, multi-channel promotional campaigns within minutes of identifying a webinar topic.
*   **Consistency & Quality:** Standardize brand voice, compliance guidelines, and high-converting copywriting frameworks across all global marketing assets.

### 1.3 Target Audience
*   **Marketing Managers:** Responsible for campaign strategy, brand alignment, and overall conversion performance.
*   **Growth & Demand Generation Marketers:** Focused on driving registrations, optimizing funnel metrics, and executing multi-channel distribution.
*   **Event Coordinators:** Tasked with the operational logistics of setting up webinars, managing attendee lists, and ensuring timely communications.

---

## 2. Scope & Core Features

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           AI Webinar Campaign Planner                           │
└────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘
                         │                        │
┌────────────────────────▼────────┐      ┌────────▼────────┐      ┌───────────────▼────────┐
│  AI Campaign Brief & Strategy   │      │  Multi-Channel  │      │  1-Click Orchestration │
│           Generator             │      │ Content Engine  │      │  & Scheduling Engine   │
└─────────────────────────────────┘      └─────────────────┘      └────────────────────────┘
```

### Feature 1: AI Campaign Brief & Strategy Generator
*   **Interactive Campaign Intake:** A structured wizard where users input basic parameters: Webinar Title, Target Audience Persona, Core Value Proposition, Date/Time, Guest Speakers, and Call to Action (CTA).
*   **AI-Generated Campaign Strategy:** The system processes inputs using a specialized LLM agent to generate a comprehensive campaign blueprint. This includes a recommended promotional timeline (e.g., T-14 days, T-7 days, T-1 day, Post-Event), distribution channel recommendations, and localized messaging angles.
*   **Dynamic Brand Voice Alignment:** Users can select from pre-configured brand profiles (e.g., Professional, Educational, Conversational) to ensure the generated strategy and subsequent assets align with corporate identity guidelines.

### Feature 2: Automated Multi-Channel Content Engine
*   **Landing Page Copy Generator:** Generates high-converting, structured landing page copy (including Hero Section, Benefits Bullet Points, Speaker Bios, and Registration Form CTAs) outputted in clean Markdown and previewable HTML.
*   **Automated Email Sequence Builder:** Generates a cohesive 5-part email sequence:
    1.  *Invitation 1 (T-14 Days)*: Educational hook and registration link.
    2.  *Invitation 2 (T-7 Days)*: Urgency-driven value proposition.
    3.  *Reminder (T-1 Day / T-1 Hour)*: Operational details and calendar invite.
    4.  *Post-Event Follow-up (Attendees)*: Recording link, key takeaways, and next-step CTA.
    5.  *Post-Event Follow-up (No-Shows)*: "Sorry we missed you" recording link and secondary CTA.
*   **Social Media Asset Suite:** Generates optimized promotional posts tailored to specific platform constraints and audiences:
    *   *LinkedIn:* Professional, long-form, value-driven posts with recommended hashtags.
    *   *Twitter/X:* Concise, hook-driven threads.
    *   *Facebook/Instagram:* Engaging, community-focused copy.
*   **Inline AI Editor:** A rich-text editor allowing users to highlight any generated text and instruct the AI to "shorten," "rewrite," "make more professional," or "translate" in real-time.

### Feature 3: 1-Click Orchestration & Scheduling Engine
*   **Marketing Automation Sync:** Direct API integration with platforms such as HubSpot, Marketo, and Customer.io to automatically create email templates, build lists, and schedule email sends.
*   **Social Media Scheduler:** Direct integration with social media APIs (LinkedIn, Twitter, Facebook) or scheduling tools (Buffer, Hootsuite) to queue promotional posts according to the generated campaign timeline.
*   **Webinar Platform Provisioning:** Integration with Zoom Video Webinars, GoToWebinar, or Teams to automatically generate the webinar event, retrieve registration links, and sync attendee data.
*   **Centralized Campaign Dashboard:** A single control room to review, edit, approve, and deploy all assets simultaneously with a single "Launch Campaign" action.

---

## 3. User Stories & Workflows

### 3.1 User Stories

| ID | User Role | Feature | Benefit |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **US-01** | Marketing Manager | AI Campaign Brief & Strategy Generator | I want to input a basic webinar title and target audience so that I can instantly generate a cohesive marketing campaign strategy and timeline without manual brainstorming. |
| **US-02** | Growth Marketer | Automated Multi-Channel Content Engine | I want the system to generate and preview landing page copy and email sequences so that I can review, edit, and approve high-quality assets in minutes instead of days. |
| **US-03** | Event Coordinator | 1-Click Orchestration & Scheduling Engine | I want to click a single "Launch Campaign" button so that all generated emails, landing pages, and social media posts are automatically scheduled and deployed across our integrated platforms. |

### 3.2 User Workflow Diagram

```
[User Input: Topic & Audience] 
       │
       ▼
[AI Generates Strategy & Assets] ──► [Interactive Review & Inline AI Editing]
                                                     │
                                                     ▼
                                        [Select Integration Channels]
                                                     │
                                                     ▼
                                        [Click "Launch Campaign"]
                                                     │
       ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
       ▼                                             ▼                                             ▼
[Create Zoom Webinar]                      [Sync HubSpot Templates]                     [Queue Social Posts]
```

### 3.3 Detailed Step-by-Step Workflow
1.  **Initiation:** The user logs into the AI Webinar Campaign Planner and clicks "Create New Campaign."
2.  **Configuration:** The user fills out the 5-field intake form (Title, Date, Speaker, Audience, CTA) and selects the "Educational" brand voice.
3.  **Generation:** The backend orchestrates parallel LLM calls to generate the campaign strategy, landing page copy, 5 emails, and 6 social media posts. This process completes in under 30 seconds.
4.  **Review & Refine:** The user reviews the generated assets side-by-side. They highlight a paragraph in Email 1 and use the inline AI prompt to "make it sound more urgent." The system updates the text instantly.
5.  **Integration Mapping:** The user toggles the active integrations: HubSpot (for emails and landing page hosting) and LinkedIn (for social posts).
6.  **Execution:** The user clicks "Launch Campaign." The system:
    *   Calls the Zoom API to create the webinar and fetches the join URL.
    *   Injects the Zoom URL into the HubSpot landing page template and publishes it.
    *   Creates and schedules the 5-part email sequence in HubSpot.
    *   Schedules the LinkedIn posts via the LinkedIn API according to the campaign timeline.
7.  **Confirmation:** The user receives a success screen displaying links to all scheduled assets and live integrations.

---

## 4. High-Level Technical Architecture

### 4.1 Technology Stack
*   **Frontend:** React.js with Next.js (App Router), Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn/ui for a responsive, accessible UI.
*   **Backend API:** Python FastAPI (high performance, native asynchronous support, excellent integration with AI libraries).
*   **Database:** PostgreSQL (relational storage for campaign metadata, user accounts, and integration tokens) + Redis (session storage, rate limiting, and Celery task queue caching).
*   **Asynchronous Task Queue:** Celery with Redis broker (handles long-running LLM generations and external API synchronization).
*   **AI Orchestration:** LangChain / LangGraph for structured agentic workflows; OpenAI GPT-4o and Anthropic Claude 3.5 Sonnet APIs (accessed via secure enterprise gateways).

### 4.2 Core Data Flow Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐             ┌─────────────┐             ┌──────────────────┐
│  Next.js UI  ├────────────►│ FastAPI API ├────────────►│ Celery Task Queue│
└──────▲───────┘             └──────┬──────┘             └────────┬─────────┘
       │                            │                             │
       │ Read/Write                 │ Read/Write                  │ Orchestrates
       │                            ▼                             ▼
┌──────┴───────┐             ┌─────────────┐             ┌──────────────────┐
│  PostgreSQL  │             │  Redis Cache│             │ LLM & Integrations│
└──────────────┘             └─────────────┘             └──────────────────┘
```

### 4.3 Integration Architecture
The platform connects to external services via secure, RESTful API integrations managed by an integration gateway service:

```
                               ┌────────────────────────┐
                               │ AI Campaign Planner    │
                               │ Integration Gateway    │
                               └───────────┬────────────┘
                                           │
                ┌──────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┐
                ▼                          ▼                          ▼
     ┌────────────────────┐     ┌────────────────────┐     ┌────────────────────┐
     │    Webinar APIs    │     │   Marketing APIs   │     │    Social APIs     │
     │  (Zoom, MS Teams)  │     │ (HubSpot, Marketo) │     │(LinkedIn, Twitter) │
     └────────────────────┘     └────────────────────┘     └────────────────────┘
```

*   **Webinar Provider Service:** Authenticates via OAuth 2.0. Creates events, updates registration settings, and retrieves unique join links.
*   **Marketing Automation Service:** Syncs HTML/Markdown templates, creates transactional email assets, and configures automated customer journeys.
*   **Social Media Publishing Service:** Handles rate-limited, scheduled POST requests to social networks, supporting text, image attachments, and link previews.

---

## 5. Security & Compliance Specifications

### 5.1 Input Validation & Prompt Defense
*   **Input Sanitization:** All user inputs are strictly sanitized on both the client and server sides using library-based HTML escaping to prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and SQL Injection.
*   **Prompt Injection Mitigation:** LLM prompts utilize strict system-message separation, XML tagging of user inputs, and defensive guardrails (e.g., NeMo Guardrails) to prevent prompt injection attacks and unauthorized system instruction overrides.
*   **Content Moderation:** Integrated automated content filtering (via OpenAI Moderation API) to detect and block hate speech, offensive content, or compliance-violating language prior to LLM processing.

### 5.2 Encryption Standards
*   **Data-in-Transit:** Enforced globally using Transport Layer Security (TLS) 1.3. HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) is enabled to prevent downgrade attacks.
*   **Data-at-Rest:** All database storage, backups, and persistent volumes are encrypted using AES-256-GCM.
*   **Secrets Management:** External API keys, OAuth client secrets, and database credentials must never be stored in the application database. They are stored in a dedicated, hardware-backed secrets manager (e.g., HashiCorp Vault or AWS Secrets Manager) and injected at runtime. Third-party OAuth tokens are encrypted at the database column level using AES-256-GCM with unique, rotating keys.

### 5.3 Access Controls & Session Management
*   **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC):** The system enforces strict RBAC with three defined roles:
    *   *Admin:* Full system configuration, integration management, and user provisioning.
    *   *Editor:* Create, edit, approve, and launch campaigns; cannot modify global integrations.
    *   *Viewer:* Read-only access to campaigns, strategies, and generated assets.
*   **Authentication:** Single Sign-On (SSO) integration via SAML 2.0 / OIDC (e.g., Okta, Azure AD). Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is mandatory for all non-SSO local accounts.
*   **Session Security:** Session tokens are stored in secure, stateful client-side cookies configured with:
    *   `HttpOnly` (prevents access via JavaScript).
    *   `Secure` (ensures transmission only over HTTPS).
    *   `SameSite=Strict` (mitigates Cross-Site Request Forgery - CSRF).
    *   Short-lived expiration (15 minutes) paired with secure sliding refresh tokens.

### 5.4 Audit Logging & Compliance
*   **Immutable Audit Logs:** Every write, update, deletion, integration toggle, and campaign launch action is logged to an immutable, append-only audit trail. Logs include timestamp, User ID, IP address, Action Type, and Affected Resource ID.
*   **OWASP Compliance:** The application architecture and deployment pipeline are designed to comply with the OWASP Top 10 (Web Application Security Risks) and the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (e.g., addressing Prompt Injection, Insecure Output Handling, and Training Data Poisoning).
*   **Data Privacy Compliance:** Full compliance with GDPR and CCPA. Users can request complete deletion of their campaign data, and no customer-specific campaign data is used to train public foundation models.

---

## 6. Key Success Metrics & KPIs

To measure the success of the AI Webinar Campaign Planner post-deployment, the following quantitative Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) will be tracked:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                              Key Success Metrics                                │
└──────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────┘
               │                        │                        │
┌──────────────▼───────┐        ┌───────▼───────┐        ┌───────▼───────┐
│   Campaign Setup     │        │    Manual     │        │  Registration │
│        Time          │        │   Overhead    │        │  Conversion   │
│  Target: <15 Minutes │        │  Target: -70% │        │  Target: ≥15% │
└──────────────────────┘        └───────────────┘        └───────────────┘
```

### 6.1 Operational Efficiency Metrics
*   **Campaign Setup Time:** The average time elapsed from initial campaign creation to full multi-channel deployment.
    *   *Baseline:* 12 hours (manual).
    *   *Target:* <15 minutes (including review and edits).
*   **Manual Overhead Reduction:** The percentage reduction in active working hours required by marketing staff to launch a single campaign.
    *   *Target:* 70% reduction in manual copywriting, formatting, and scheduling tasks.
*   **Time-to-Market (TTM):** The duration between identifying a trending topic and launching the corresponding promotional campaign.
    *   *Target:* Under 1 hour.

### 6.2 Platform Engagement & Adoption Metrics
*   **User Adoption Rate:** The percentage of internal marketing and growth teams actively using the platform to orchestrate webinar campaigns.
    *   *Target:* 85% adoption within 90 days of deployment.
*   **1-Click Launch Success Rate:** The percentage of initiated campaign launches that successfully deploy to all selected integrations (HubSpot, Zoom, LinkedIn) without API errors.
    *   *Target:* ≥99.5% success rate.
*   **AI Asset Utilization:** The percentage of AI-generated copy that is published with minimal manual modification (less than 20% of the text altered).
    *   *Target:* ≥75% of generated assets.

### 6.3 Marketing Performance Metrics
*   **Registration-to-Attendee Conversion Rate:** The percentage of registered users who attend the live webinar.
    *   *Target:* Maintain or exceed the historical baseline of 15% to 20%, proving that AI-generated copy matches or exceeds human-written copy quality.
*   **Click-Through Rate (CTR) on AI Emails:** The percentage of email recipients who click the registration link.
    *   *Target:* Maintain or exceed the historical baseline of 3.5% CTR.

# Product Requirements Document (PRD): AI Partner Risk Detection

## 1. Executive Overview & Goals

### 1.1 Executive Summary
In today's highly regulated global market, organizations face severe financial, legal, and reputational risks when engaging with third-party trading partners. Traditional partner auditing is a reactive, manual, and slow process, often taking days or weeks to uncover compliance infractions, sanctions, or adverse news. 

The **AI Partner Risk Detection** system is an enterprise-grade, proactive risk mitigation platform. By leveraging agentic news screening, real-time regulatory updates, and machine learning-driven behavioral history analysis, the platform continuously monitors and flags high-risk business partners *before* manual audits occur. This shifts the compliance paradigm from periodic manual sampling to continuous, real-time automated vigilance.

### 1.2 Business Goals & Strategic Impact
*   **Mitigate Compliance & Legal Exposure:** Protect the firm from catastrophic regulatory breaches, OFAC/sanctions violations, and multi-million dollar fines.
*   **Enhance Operational Efficiency:** Reduce partner background research and vetting times from hours to seconds, allowing compliance teams to focus on high-value investigation rather than manual data gathering.
*   **Build Platform Trust & Integrity:** Establish a gold standard of partner integrity, directly boosting customer confidence, investor trust, and long-term brand equity.
*   **Proactive Risk Management:** Identify behavioral anomalies and emerging risks before they manifest as active legal liabilities.

---

## 2. Scope & Core Features

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           AI Partner Risk Detection                             │
└────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘
                         │                       │
                         ▼                       ▼
┌────────────────────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────────────────┐
│ 1. Agentic News & Reg Engine   │ │ 2. Behavioral Risk Scoring   │ │ 3. Real-Time Alerting Dash  │
│ • Multi-source ingestion       │ │ • Historical pattern analysis│ │ • Interactive risk profiles │
│ • LLM-based entity resolution  │ │ • Dynamic risk scores (0-100)│ │ • Human-in-the-loop actions │
│ • Adverse sentiment analysis   │ │ • Explainable AI indicators  │ │ • Webhook & SIEM integration│
└────────────────────────────────┘ └──────────────────────────────┘ └─────────────────────────────┘
```

### Feature 1: Agentic Real-Time News & Regulatory Screening Engine
*   **Continuous Multi-Source Ingestion:** Automated ingestion of global news feeds, regulatory updates (SEC, FinCEN, OFAC, European Commission, etc.), and international sanctions watchlists.
*   **Entity Resolution & Disambiguation:** Advanced LLM-driven entity resolution to ensure adverse news is accurately mapped to the correct partner, preventing false positives caused by common names.
*   **Semantic Sentiment & Risk Extraction:** Agentic analysis of unstructured text to classify risks into specific taxonomies (e.g., Bribery & Corruption, Modern Slavery, Environmental Violations, Financial Instability).

### Feature 2: Behavioral History & Risk Scoring Model
*   **Historical Pattern Analysis:** Machine learning models that analyze transactional history, contract deviations, late payment patterns, and past compliance infractions.
*   **Dynamic Risk Scoring:** Generation of a real-time "Partner Risk Score" (0 to 100) categorized into Low, Medium, and High risk tiers.
*   **Explainable AI (XAI) Indicators:** Every risk score is accompanied by a natural language summary explaining *why* the score was generated, highlighting the specific data points and weights used.

### Feature 3: Real-Time Alerting & Incident Management Dashboard
*   **Instant Alerting Pipeline:** Real-time notifications dispatched via Webhooks, Email, Slack, Microsoft Teams, or direct integration with SIEM/SOAR platforms when a partner's risk score crosses a defined threshold.
*   **Interactive Risk Profiles:** A centralized dashboard displaying a 360-degree view of each partner, including historical risk trends, recent news sentiment, and active warning flags.
*   **Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Workflows:** Capabilities for compliance officers to assign alerts, log manual review notes, override risk scores with documented justification, and initiate formal audit escalations.

---

## 3. User Stories & Workflows

### 3.1 User Stories

| ID | As a... | I want to... | So that... |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **US-01** | Compliance Analyst | Receive real-time alerts when an active trading partner is added to a global sanctions list or mentioned in adverse regulatory news | I can immediately freeze transactions and prevent regulatory violations. |
| **US-02** | Procurement Manager | View a comprehensive AI-generated risk profile and historical behavior score of a prospective partner during onboarding | I can make informed vetting decisions without waiting days for manual background checks. |
| **US-03** | Chief Compliance Officer | Access an automated, immutable audit trail of all risk assessments, flag dismissals, and escalation actions | I can demonstrate rigorous regulatory compliance to external auditors and regulatory bodies. |

### 3.2 User Workflow: Adverse Event Detection to Resolution

```
[ External Adverse Event ] ──> ( Ingestion & Entity Resolution )
                                              │
                                              ▼
                                 ( AI Risk Score Recalculation )
                                              │
                                              ▼
                                 [ Risk Threshold Exceeded ]
                                              │
                                              ▼
                                  ( Real-Time Alert Sent )
                                              │
                                              ▼
                                 [ Analyst Reviews Dashboard ]
                                              │
                       ┌──────────────────────┴──────────────────────┐
                       ▼                                             ▼
             [ Dismiss Alert ]                              [ Escalate Alert ]
         (Log Justification -> Logged)                 (Freeze Partner -> Audit Initiated)
```

1.  **Ingestion:** The system detects a new regulatory filing indicating a trading partner is under investigation for bribery.
2.  **Analysis:** The AI engine resolves the entity, recalculates the partner's Risk Score from 24 (Low) to 82 (High), and generates a natural language summary of the risk.
3.  **Alerting:** An automated high-priority alert is dispatched to the Compliance team's dashboard and Slack channel.
4.  **Triage:** A Compliance Analyst opens the dashboard, reviews the AI-generated summary, and examines the linked source documents.
5.  **Resolution:** The analyst escalates the issue, triggering an automated API call to the internal ERP system to temporarily freeze active purchase orders with the partner pending a formal audit.

---

## 4. High-Level Technical Architecture

### 4.1 Technical Stack
*   **Frontend:** React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vite (optimized for fast, responsive dashboard rendering).
*   **Backend Services:** FastAPI (Python) for high-performance, asynchronous API endpoints; Go for high-throughput data ingestion pipelines.
*   **Data Tier:**
    *   *PostgreSQL:* Relational database for structured partner metadata, user accounts, and audit logs.
    *   *Pinecone / Milvus:* Vector database for storing semantic embeddings of news articles and regulatory filings to enable rapid similarity searches.
    *   *Redis:* In-memory cache for session management and message queuing (Celery).
*   **AI/ML Engine:** 
    *   LlamaIndex / LangChain for agentic orchestration.
    *   GPT-4o / Claude 3.5 Sonnet (via secure, private API endpoints) for complex reasoning and synthesis.
    *   HuggingFace Transformers (local deployment) for high-speed Named Entity Recognition (NER) and initial classification.

### 4.2 Core Data Flow Architecture

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                     DATA SOURCES                                       │
│  ┌────────────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────┐  │

```

### 4.3 External Integrations
*   **Compliance Data Providers:** Integrations with Dow Jones Risk & Compliance, OpenSanctions, and LexisNexis APIs.
*   **Internal Systems:** Bi-directional REST APIs with SAP, Oracle NetSuite, and Salesforce to pull partner directories and push risk status updates.

---

## 5. Security & Compliance Specifications

### 5.1 Input Validation & Sanitization
*   **Strict Schema Validation:** All incoming API payloads must be validated against strict JSON schemas using Pydantic (Python) and API Gateway validation models.
*   **Prompt Injection Mitigation:** For LLM-based processing, strict system prompt isolation, input length constraints, and output validation (using guardrails like NeMo Guardrails) are enforced to prevent prompt injection attacks.
*   **Sanitization:** All user-generated inputs (e.g., audit notes, override justifications) are sanitized to prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and SQL Injection (SQLi).

### 5.2 Encryption Standards
*   **Data-at-Rest:** All persistent data stores (PostgreSQL, Vector DB, backups) are encrypted using **AES-256-GCM**. Encryption keys are managed via a secure Cloud Key Management Service (KMS) with automatic rotation.
*   **Data-in-Transit:** All network communications, both external and internal (microservice-to-microservice), are strictly enforced using **TLS 1.3**. Legacy TLS versions (1.1 and below) are explicitly disabled.

### 5.3 Access Controls & Session Management
*   **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC):** Granular permissions mapped to specific user roles:
    *   `Viewer`: Can view partner risk scores and profiles.
    *   `Analyst`: Can triage alerts, add notes, and initiate escalations.
    *   `Admin`: Can modify risk thresholds, manage users, and override risk scores.
*   **Session Security:** Authentication tokens (JWT) are stored exclusively in **`HttpOnly`**, **`Secure`**, and **`SameSite=Strict`** cookies to prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) token theft.
*   **Single Sign-On (SSO):** Integration with enterprise Identity Providers (IdPs) via SAML 2.0 or OIDC (e.g., Okta, Azure AD) with mandatory Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA).

### 5.4 Audit Logging & Compliance
*   **Immutable Audit Trail:** All state-changing actions (e.g., risk score overrides, alert dismissals, user login attempts) are logged to an append-only, write-once-read-many (WORM) storage system.
*   **Log Structure:** Every log entry must contain: Timestamp (UTC), User ID, IP Address, Action Performed, Affected Entity, and Pre/Post State values.
*   **OWASP Compliance:** The application is architected and regularly tested against the **OWASP Top 10** vulnerabilities. Annual third-party penetration testing is mandatory.

---

## 6. Key Success Metrics & KPIs

To measure the effectiveness and operational impact of the AI Partner Risk Detection platform, the following key performance indicators (KPIs) will be tracked:

| Metric Name | Target | Measurement Methodology |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Mean Time to Detect (MTTD)** | < 15 Minutes | Measured from the exact timestamp a regulatory update or adverse news article is published online to the timestamp the risk alert is generated in the system. |
| **False Positive Rate (FPR)** | < 12% | Percentage of automated alerts flagged as "High Risk" that are subsequently dismissed as irrelevant or incorrect by compliance analysts during triage. |
| **Audit Efficiency Gain** | > 90% Time Reduction | Comparison of average time spent by analysts conducting manual background research per partner (Baseline: 4 hours) vs. reviewing the AI-generated risk profile (Target: < 5 minutes). |
| **Continuous Monitoring Coverage** | 100% | Percentage of active, onboarded trading partners continuously monitored by the real-time screening engine. |
| **System Adoption Rate** | > 85% | Percentage of daily active compliance team members utilizing the platform as their primary triage tool. |

# Product Requirements Document (PRD): Smart CRM Segmentation

## 1. Executive Overview & Goals

### 1.1 Executive Summary
Modern marketing campaigns suffer from low engagement and high opt-out rates due to generic, non-personalized messaging. The **Smart CRM Segmentation** engine solves this by leveraging predictive machine learning models to dynamically analyze customer behavioral data, automatically group users into high-affinity cohorts, and trigger hyper-targeted promotional campaigns. By shifting from static, rule-based lists to real-time, predictive behavioral clustering, this system aims to significantly improve customer retention and maximize lifetime value (LTV).

### 1.2 Business Objectives
*   **Reactivate Dormant Users:** Identify slipping or dormant users before they churn and automatically deliver tailored re-engagement offers.
*   **Improve Campaign Performance:** Increase email and push notification open-rates by **25%** through hyper-relevant targeting.
*   **Reduce Operational Overhead:** Automate the segmentation pipeline, reducing the manual effort required by marketing teams to plan, extract, and execute targeted campaigns.

### 1.3 Target Audience
*   **Marketing Managers & Growth Marketers:** Primary operators who design, execute, and monitor campaigns.
*   **Data Scientists & ML Engineers:** Technical stakeholders who configure, evaluate, and tune the predictive models.
*   **End Customers:** Platform users who receive highly contextualized, non-spammy, and timely offers.

---

## 2. Scope & Core Features

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           SMART CRM SEGMENTATION ENGINE                         │
├─────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────┤
│   1. PREDICTIVE ENGINE  │   2. DYNAMIC SYNC       │   3. CAMPAIGN DASHBOARD     │
│   • RFM Clustering      │   • Real-Time Triggers  │   • Offer Mapping           │
│   • PII Anonymization   │   • Downstream Sync     │   • A/B Testing Framework   │
└─────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┘
```

### Feature 1: Predictive Behavioral Clustering Engine
*   **Description:** An unsupervised and supervised machine learning pipeline that processes historical CRM data, transactional records, and real-time event streams to classify users into dynamic behavioral cohorts.
*   **Key Capabilities:**
    *   **RFM Analysis:** Automatically calculates Recency, Frequency, and Monetary values for every customer profile.
    *   **Predictive Churn Scoring:** Assigns a real-time probability score indicating the likelihood of a user becoming dormant within the next 14 to 30 days.
    *   **Automated Re-Clustering:** Runs daily batch jobs to re-evaluate user profiles and transition them between segments (e.g., transitioning a user from "Active Enthusiast" to "Slipping Customer").

### Feature 2: Dynamic Segment Sync & Real-Time Triggering
*   **Description:** A synchronization pipeline that pushes updated segment memberships to downstream marketing automation platforms (e.g., Braze, HubSpot, Salesforce Marketing Cloud) and triggers instant actions.
*   **Key Capabilities:**
    *   **Transition Triggers:** Fires webhooks or event messages the moment a user transitions into a critical segment (e.g., entering the "At-Risk Dormant" segment triggers an immediate high-value discount offer).
    *   **Differential Syncing:** Optimizes API usage by syncing only delta changes (adds/removes) to external CRMs rather than full database dumps.

### Feature 3: Automated Campaign & Offer Personalization Dashboard
*   **Description:** A unified marketer-facing user interface to map specific promotional offers, discount codes, or content templates to dynamically generated segments.
*   **Key Capabilities:**
    *   **Offer Mapping Matrix:** Allows non-technical marketers to drag and drop promotional assets onto predictive segments.
    *   **A/B Testing Framework:** Splits segments into test and control groups to measure the exact lift in open-rates, click-through rates (CTR), and conversion rates driven by the predictive models.
    *   **Performance Analytics:** Displays real-time dashboards showing open-rates, conversion rates, and estimated revenue generated per segment.

### Feature 4: Privacy-Preserving Data Anonymization Pipeline
*   **Description:** A secure pre-processing pipeline that strips and tokenizes Personally Identifiable Information (PII) before data is ingested by the machine learning models.
*   **Key Capabilities:**
    *   **One-Way Hashing:** Hashes identifiers (emails, phone numbers, user IDs) using SHA-256 with a rotating salt.
    *   **Data Minimization:** Excludes sensitive fields (e.g., physical addresses, full names, billing details) from the ML feature store, feeding only behavioral vectors into the clustering algorithms.

---

## 3. User Stories & Workflows

### 3.1 User Story 1: Automated Re-Engagement Campaign
*   **As a** Marketing Manager,
*   **I want** the system to automatically identify users who are at risk of churning and assign them to a "Slipping" segment,
*   **So that** I can target them with a personalized re-engagement offer without having to manually pull SQL queries or export CSV files.
*   **Acceptance Criteria:**
    *   The system must update the "Slipping" segment daily.
    *   The transition of a user into the "Slipping" segment must trigger an automated API call to the Email Service Provider (ESP) within 15 minutes.
    *   The marketer must be able to view the conversion rate of this automated campaign directly on the dashboard.

### 3.2 User Story 2: Model Tuning and Validation
*   **As a** Data Scientist,
*   **I want** to evaluate the silhouette score and cluster stability of the predictive models through an administrative interface,
*   **So that** I can ensure the segments remain statistically distinct and accurate over time.
*   **Acceptance Criteria:**
    *   The admin panel must display the silhouette score, Davies-Bouldin index, and cluster sizes after every model training run.
    *   The system must trigger an alert to the engineering team if the silhouette score drops below 0.50.
    *   The administrator must have the ability to roll back to a previous model version with a single click.

### 3.3 User Story 3: Receiving Personalized Offers
*   **As a** Platform Customer,
*   **I want** to receive promotional offers that match my actual usage history and preferences,
*   **So that** I am not spammed with irrelevant marketing emails.
*   **Acceptance Criteria:**
    *   The customer must only receive offers mapped to their active behavioral segment.
    *   If a customer's behavior changes (e.g., they resume high-frequency usage), they must immediately stop receiving "we miss you" discount emails.

---

## 4. High-Level Technical Architecture

The architecture is designed for high scalability, low latency, and strict data privacy. It decouples the heavy machine learning computations from the transactional CRM database.

```
                                 [ RAW DATA SOURCES ]
                       (CRM DB, Transaction Logs, Event Streams)
                                          │
                                          ▼
                       ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
                       │  PII Anonymization & Tokenization    │
                       └──────────────────────────────────────┘
                                          │
                                          ▼
                       ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
                       │      Feature Store (RFM Metrics)     │
                       └──────────────────────────────────────┘
                                          │
                                          ▼
                       ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
                       │     ML Clustering Engine (Airflow)   │
                       └──────────────────────────────────────┘
                                          │
                                          ▼
                       ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
                       │     Segment Assignment Database      │
                       └──────────────────────────────────────┘
                                          │
                     ┌────────────────────┴────────────────────┐
                     ▼                                         ▼
        ┌─────────────────────────┐               ┌─────────────────────────┐
        │   Downstream Sync API   │               │   Marketer Dashboard    │
        │  (Braze, HubSpot, etc.) │               │      (React / Go)       │
        └─────────────────────────┘               └─────────────────────────┘
```

### 4.1 Technical Stack
*   **Data Ingestion & Pipeline:** Apache Kafka (real-time event streaming), Apache Airflow (batch orchestration), Snowflake (data warehousing).
*   **Machine Learning Framework:** Python, Scikit-Learn (K-Means, DBSCAN for clustering), XGBoost (for churn prediction), MLflow (model registry and tracking).
*   **Backend Services:** Go (Golang) microservices for high-performance segment querying and downstream API synchronization.
*   **Frontend UI:** React.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Tremor (for analytics visualization).
*   **Database:** PostgreSQL (for campaign metadata and application state), Redis (for caching active segments and user states).

### 4.2 Core Data Flow
1.  **Ingestion & Anonymization:** Raw transactional and behavioral events are ingested via Kafka. The *Anonymization Pipeline* strips PII, hashes identifiers, and writes clean behavioral records to the Snowflake Data Warehouse.
2.  **Feature Extraction:** Airflow orchestrates daily jobs to calculate RFM features and behavioral vectors, saving them to the Feature Store.
3.  **Model Execution:** The ML Engine runs clustering and churn prediction models against the Feature Store, generating segment assignments and churn probability scores.
4.  **Database Update:** Updated segment memberships are written to the PostgreSQL *Segment Assignment Database*.
5.  **Downstream Sync:** The *Sync Service* detects changes in segment membership and pushes updates to external marketing platforms (e.g., Braze, Salesforce) via secure REST APIs.

---

## 5. Security & Compliance Specifications

Security is a primary design constraint. The system strictly isolates customer identity from predictive modeling to ensure compliance with global privacy standards.

### 5.1 Encryption Standards
*   **Data at Rest:** All databases, caches, and storage volumes must be encrypted using **AES-256-GCM**. Encryption keys are managed and rotated automatically via AWS KMS or HashiCorp Vault.
*   **Data in Transit:** All network communication, including internal microservice-to-microservice traffic and external API calls, must enforce **TLS 1.3** (with fallback to TLS 1.2 using secure cipher suites).

### 5.2 Input Validation & Sanitization
*   All incoming API payloads must undergo strict schema validation using JSON Schema or Zod libraries.
*   Strict sanitization rules are applied to prevent SQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), and Remote Code Execution (RCE).

### 5.3 Access Controls & Session Management
*   **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC):** Fine-grained permissions must be enforced.
    *   `Marketer`: Can view segments, map campaigns, and view analytics. Cannot modify ML model configurations or access raw database tables.
    *   `Data Scientist`: Can register models, adjust hyperparameters, and view model performance metrics.
    *   `System Admin`: Full read/write access to system configurations, integrations, and user management.
*   **Session Security:** User sessions are managed via JSON Web Tokens (JWT) signed with RS256. Tokens must be stored in **HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite=Strict** cookies to mitigate token theft and XSS attacks.

### 5.4 Audit Logging & Compliance
*   **Immutable Audit Logs:** Every write, update, delete, and segment export action must be logged to an immutable, write-once-read-many (WORM) storage system (e.g., AWS S3 with Object Lock).
*   **OWASP Compliance:** The application must comply with the OWASP Top 10 security standards. Regular automated SAST (Static Application Security Testing) and DAST (Dynamic Application Security Testing) scans must be integrated into the CI/CD pipeline.
*   **Regulatory Compliance:** The system must fully comply with **GDPR** and **CCPA**. It must support the "Right to be Forgotten" (Data Erasure) by purging user records from both the transactional database and the ML feature store within 72 hours of a request.

---

## 6. Key Success Metrics & KPIs

To evaluate the performance and business impact of the Smart CRM Segmentation engine, the following key performance indicators will be tracked continuously:

| Metric Name | Target | Measurement Methodology |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Campaign Open-Rate Lift** | **+25% relative increase** | Measured via continuous A/B testing comparing predictive segments against legacy static/rule-based lists. |
| **Dormant User Reactivation Rate** | **+15% increase** | Percentage of users in the "At-Risk/Dormant" segment who perform a key conversion action within 14 days of receiving a targeted offer. |
| **Campaign Setup Efficiency** | **< 1 hour per week** | Average time spent by marketing managers creating, exporting, and uploading segments to marketing tools (Target: 90% reduction from baseline of 12 hours/week). |
| **Model Accuracy & Stability** | **Silhouette Score > 0.55** | Monitored daily in the MLflow registry to ensure clusters remain mathematically distinct and do not degrade. |
| **Sync Latency** | **< 15 minutes** | The time elapsed between a user transitioning segments in the database and the update reflecting in downstream marketing platforms. |

# Product Requirements Document (PRD): AI Partner Health & Risk Monitor

## 1. Executive Overview & Goals

### 1.1. Executive Summary
The **AI Partner Health & Risk Monitor** is an enterprise-grade, proactive intelligence platform designed to continuously analyze partner activity, detect anomalies, and predict churn risks before they impact top-line revenue. 

Currently, Partner Managers (PMs) manually monitor partner performance across multiple siloed dashboards and static reports. This reactive approach leads to delayed interventions, allowing declining partner activity, abnormal traffic patterns, and early signs of churn to go unnoticed until significant business impact has occurred. 

By consolidating disparate real-time data streams into a unified machine learning pipeline, this system automates anomaly detection and generates proactive, actionable alerts. This shifts the partner management paradigm from reactive firefighting to proactive, high-impact relationship management.

### 1.2. Business Goals & Objectives
*   **Protect Partner-Driven Revenue:** Mitigate the financial risk of sudden partner churn, stabilizing top-line revenue streams.
*   **Enhance Operational Efficiency:** Eliminate manual dashboard scanning, reducing cognitive fatigue and allowing PMs to focus on high-value strategic interactions.
*   **Improve Partner Retention:** Identify struggling partners early to enable targeted, timely interventions.
*   **Optimize Resource Allocation:** Direct account management resources toward high-potential partners showing strong growth trends.

### 1.3. Target Users
*   **Partner Managers (PMs):** Primary users who act on alerts, review partner health scores, and execute recommended actions.
*   **Account Directors / VP of Partnerships:** Strategic users who monitor macro-level partner ecosystem health, revenue protection metrics, and team response times.
*   **Risk & Compliance Officers:** Secondary users who monitor traffic quality anomalies, potential fraud spikes, and ensure regulatory compliance.

---

## 2. Scope & Core Features

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          AI Partner Health & Risk Monitor                       │
├────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┤
│    1. Data Ingestion   │     2. AI Analytics    │     3. Alerting & Actions     │
│  • Multi-stream ETL    │  • Anomaly Detection   │  • Proactive Alerts           │
│  • Real-time Latency   │  • Churn Prediction    │  • Next-Best-Action Engine    │
│  • Data Masking        │  • Health Scoring      │  • CRM & Slack Integrations   │
└────────────────────────┴────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────┘
```

### Feature 1: Real-Time Multi-Stream Data Ingestion & Aggregation Pipeline
*   **Continuous Ingestion:** Consolidates disparate data streams into a unified, low-latency data pipeline.
*   **Monitored Metrics:**
    *   *Registrations:* Hourly and daily partner-referred registration volumes.
    *   *Deposits:* Transactional volume, frequency, and net deposit values.
    *   *Trading Volume:* Real-time asset/contract trading volumes generated by partner networks.
    *   *Conversion Rates:* Click-to-registration and registration-to-deposit ratios.
    *   *Campaign Engagement:* Partner interaction with marketing assets, email campaigns, and portal logins.
    *   *Traffic Quality:* IP distribution, device fingerprints, bounce rates, and bot-likelihood scores.
*   **Latency Management:** Utilizes a stream-processing architecture to ensure data latency from source to inference engine is under 5 minutes.

### Feature 2: AI-Powered Anomaly Detection & Predictive Risk Engine
*   **Dynamic Baselines:** Replaces static thresholds with dynamic, partner-specific historical baselines using time-series forecasting models.
*   **Anomaly Detection:** Automatically flags statistical deviations (e.g., sudden registration spikes, abrupt drops in trading volume).
*   **Predictive Churn Scoring:** Calculates a daily "Partner Health Score" (0-100) using a supervised machine learning model that weighs declining engagement, conversion drops, and deposit patterns.
*   **Growth Trend Identification:** Identifies high-potential partners exhibiting positive velocity in key metrics to highlight expansion opportunities.

### Feature 3: Proactive Alerting & Actionable Recommendation Engine
*   **Automated Alert Generation:** Triggers real-time alerts for critical events, including:
    *   *Activity Decline:* Partner activity or trading volume declining by $\ge 30\%$ against their 14-day rolling baseline.
    *   *Unusual Spikes:* Rapid, abnormal registration spikes indicating potential marketing fraud or viral campaigns.
    *   *Growth Trends:* High-potential partners showing sustained growth trends over a 7-day period.
*   **Next-Best-Action (NBA) Generator:** Leverages a hybrid rule-based and LLM-powered recommendation engine to append contextual, actionable steps to every alert (e.g., *"Offer Tier-2 commission incentive,"* *"Schedule immediate health check call,"* or *"Flag accounts for compliance review"*).

### Feature 4: Unified Partner Health Dashboard & Workspace
*   **Single Pane of Glass:** A centralized web interface displaying the global ecosystem health, active alerts, and prioritized partner lists.
*   **Partner Profile View:** Deep-dive visualization of individual partner health trends, historical anomalies, and audit logs of past interventions.
*   **Action Center:** Interface for PMs to claim alerts, log actions taken, snooze alerts with justification, or escalate issues to compliance.

---

## 3. User Stories & Workflows

### 3.1. User Stories

| ID | As a... | I want to... | So that... |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **US-01** | Partner Manager | Receive an immediate, high-priority alert when a high-value partner's trading volume drops by 30% compared to their rolling baseline, accompanied by recommended intervention steps | I can proactively contact the partner and resolve their issues before they churn. |
| **US-02** | Account Director | View a prioritized list of high-potential partners showing positive growth trends and high conversion rates | I can strategically allocate co-marketing budgets and account management resources to maximize ROI. |
| **US-03** | Risk & Compliance Officer | Be automatically notified of abnormal registration spikes or suspicious traffic patterns with masked sensitive financial data | I can investigate potential affiliate fraud or compliance violations without exposing sensitive partner PII. |

### 3.2. Operational Workflow: Churn Risk Mitigation

```
[Partner Activity Drops 30%] ──> [Ingestion Pipeline] ──> [AI Risk Engine Flags Anomaly]
                                                                   │
[Partner Manager Resolves Alert] <── [PM Reviews NBA & Contacts] <── [Alert Sent to Slack/CRM]
```

1.  **Detection:** The ingestion pipeline processes a sudden 35% drop in trading volume and a 40% drop in deposits over a 48-hour period for *Partner X* (a Tier-1 partner).
2.  **Analysis:** The AI Risk Engine flags this anomaly, drops *Partner X's* Health Score from 85 to 42, and classifies them as "High Churn Risk."
3.  **Alert Generation:** The Recommendation Engine generates an alert: *"High Risk: Partner X activity declined by 35%."* It appends the recommendation: *"Decline correlates with a drop in conversion rate on Campaign Y. Action: Contact Partner X to verify if tracking links are broken, and offer a temporary 5% rebate boost."*
4.  **Notification:** The alert is pushed to the Partner Manager's Slack channel and logged in the CRM.
5.  **Triage & Action:** The PM clicks the alert, reviews the masked financial data on the dashboard, and clicks "Execute Recommendation." The system pre-drafts an email with the diagnostic details.
6.  **Resolution:** The PM contacts the partner, identifies a broken tracking link on the partner's site, assists in fixing it, and logs the resolution in the system. The partner's health score recovers over the next 72 hours.

---

## 4. High-Level Technical Architecture

The system is designed as a highly scalable, event-driven microservices architecture to handle high-throughput financial and transactional data streams with minimal latency.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                   TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE                                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

   [Data Sources] (Registrations, Deposits, Trades, Traffic)
         │
         ▼
   ┌───────────────┐      ┌──────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────┐
   │  Apache Kafka │ ───> │ Apache Flink     │ ───> │ TimescaleDB      │ (Time-Series Store)
   └───────────────┘      │ (Stream Process) │      └──────────────────┘
                          └──────────────────┘                │
                                    │                         ▼
                                    ▼               ┌──────────────────┐
                          ┌──────────────────┐      │ PostgreSQL       │ (Metadata & Config)
                          │ ML Inference     │ <─── └──────────────────┘
                          │ (Python/XGBoost) │
                          └──────────────────┘
                                    │
                                    ▼
                          ┌──────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────┐
                          │ Alert & NBA      │ ───> │ Redis            │ (Caching & Session)
                          │ Engine (Node.js) │      └──────────────────┘
                          └──────────────────┘
                                    │
         ┌──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┐
         ▼                                                     ▼
   ┌───────────────┐                                     ┌───────────────┐
   │ React Frontend│                                     │ External APIs │
   │ (Dashboard)   │                                     │ (Slack, CRM)  │
   └───────────────┘                                     └───────────────┘
```

### 4.1. Technical Stack
*   **Data Ingestion & Streaming:** Apache Kafka (Event Streaming), Apache Flink (Real-time Stream Processing).
*   **Databases:** TimescaleDB (Time-series metrics), PostgreSQL (Structured metadata, configurations, and audit logs), Redis (Caching and session management).
*   **Machine Learning Pipeline:** Python, Scikit-Learn, XGBoost (for tabular anomaly detection and churn prediction), PyTorch (for sequence-based time-series forecasting).
*   **Backend Services:** Node.js / NestJS (API Gateway, Alerting Engine, and Next-Best-Action service).
*   **Frontend:** React.js with TypeScript, TailwindCSS, and Tremor (for analytical UI components).

### 4.2. Core Data Flows
1.  **Ingestion Flow:** Raw transactional and behavioral events are published to Kafka topics (`partner.registrations`, `partner.deposits`, `partner.trades`, `partner.traffic`).
2.  **Processing Flow:** Apache Flink aggregates these events into sliding windows (e.g., 1-hour, 24-hour, 7-day) and writes the aggregated metrics to TimescaleDB.
3.  **Inference Flow:** A lightweight Python microservice pulls aggregated features from TimescaleDB hourly, runs the anomaly detection and churn prediction models, and writes updated health scores and anomaly flags back to PostgreSQL.
4.  **Alerting Flow:** If an anomaly or high-risk threshold is breached, the Alerting Engine generates an alert payload, queries the Recommendation Engine for the next-best-action, and pushes the enriched alert to the React Frontend via WebSockets and external integrations via webhooks.

### 4.3. External Integrations
*   **CRM Integration:** Bi-directional sync with Salesforce and HubSpot APIs to log alerts as tasks and update partner account statuses.
*   **Notification Gateways:** Slack Webhooks and Microsoft Teams Graph API for real-time channel alerts; SendGrid for automated email notifications.
*   **Partner Portal API:** Integration with the core partner portal to fetch campaign engagement metrics and display health-related feedback directly to partners if configured.

---

## 5. Security & Compliance Specifications

Given that the system monitors sensitive financial data (deposits, trading volumes) and partner activities, strict security controls are required to mitigate compliance risks and prevent unauthorized data exposure.

### 5.1. Encryption Standards
*   **Data in Transit:** All external and internal microservice communications must use **TLS 1.3**. Legacy TLS versions (1.1 and below) must be explicitly disabled.
*   **Data at Rest:** All databases, caches, and storage volumes must be encrypted using **AES-256-GCM**. Encryption keys must be managed and rotated automatically via an enterprise Key Management Service (KMS).

### 5.2. Access Controls & Session Management
*   **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC):** Strict access levels must be enforced at the API gateway level:

| Role | Dashboard Access | Financial Data Visibility | Alert Resolution | System Config |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Partner Manager** | Read/Write (Assigned Only) | Masked | Yes | No |
| **Account Director** | Read/Write (All) | Full | Yes | No |
| **Compliance Officer**| Read-Only (All) | Full | No | No |
| **System Admin** | Read/Write (All) | Masked | No | Yes |

*   **Session Security:** 
    *   Authentication tokens must be issued as JSON Web Tokens (JWT) stored in **HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite=Strict** cookies to prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) attacks.
    *   Session lifetimes must be capped at 12 hours, requiring re-authentication.

### 5.3. Data Masking & Privacy Compliance
*   **PII & Financial Masking:** Raw deposit amounts, trading volumes, and partner personal identifiable information (PII) must be masked for non-authorized roles. 
    *   *Example:* A deposit of `$124,500.00` is displayed to a standard Partner Manager as `$120k+` or represented as a normalized index score (e.g., *Volume Index: 8.4*).
*   **GDPR/CCPA Compliance:** The system must support "Right to be Forgotten" requests. A hard-delete routine must be available to purge or anonymize all historical activity logs associated with a specific partner within 30 days of a request.

### 5.4. Audit Logging & OWASP Compliance
*   **Immutable Audit Logs:** All user actions—including alert views, alert resolutions, configuration changes, and data exports—must be logged to an append-only, write-once-read-many (WORM) log stream in PostgreSQL, replicated to a secure cloud storage bucket.
*   **OWASP Top 10 Mitigation:**
    *   *Injection:* Use parameterized queries and Object-Relational Mapping (ORM) tools exclusively.
    *   *Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA):* Validate that the authenticated user has explicit ownership/access rights to the requested partner ID on every API request.
    *   *Rate Limiting:* Enforce API rate limits (e.g., maximum 100 requests per minute per user) to prevent Denial of Service (DoS) and brute-force attempts.

---

## 6. Key Success Metrics & KPIs

To evaluate the performance, adoption, and business impact of the AI Partner Health & Risk Monitor, the following metrics will be tracked continuously:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                   KEY SUCCESS METRICS                                   │
├───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┤
│     Financial Impact      │    Operational Efficiency   │       Model Performance       │
│  • Churn Rate: -15%       │  • MTTD: < 1 Hour           │  • Precision: > 85%           │
│  • Revenue Saved: Tracked │  • MTTR: < 24 Hours         │  • Recall: > 90%              │
└───────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────┘
```

### 6.1. Financial & Retention Metrics
*   **Partner Churn Rate Reduction:** Target a **$15\%$ relative reduction** in annual partner churn within 6 months of deployment.
*   **Revenue Protected:** Total revenue generated by partners who were flagged as "High Risk" but successfully retained post-intervention, measured quarterly.
*   **Partner Lifetime Value (LTV) Increase:** Average increase in LTV for partners managed via the proactive alerting system versus the historical manual baseline.

### 6.2. Operational Efficiency Metrics
*   **Mean Time to Detect (MTTD):** Average time elapsed from the occurrence of an anomaly (e.g., volume drop) to the generation of the alert. Target: **$< 1$ hour** (down from days/weeks).
*   **Mean Time to Respond (MTTR):** Average time taken by a Partner Manager to claim and act on an alert. Target: **$< 24$ hours**.
*   **Cognitive Load Reduction:** Time spent by PMs manually reviewing performance dashboards. Target: **$70\%$ reduction** in manual dashboard review hours.

### 6.3. System & AI Accuracy Metrics
*   **Model Precision:** Percentage of generated alerts that represent true anomalies or actual risks. Target: **$> 85\%$** (to prevent alert fatigue).
*   **Model Recall:** Percentage of actual partner churn events or significant drops that were successfully predicted by the system at least 14 days in advance. Target: **$> 90\%$**.
*   **Recommendation Acceptance Rate:** Percentage of AI-generated Next-Best-Actions executed by PMs without modification. Target: **$> 65\%$**.

phases.title: Requirements, Architecture & LLM Integration
deliverables: LLM prompt templates for emails, landing pages, and social posts
API integration specifications for generative AI services
tasks: Define database schema for campaign metadata and generated assets
Set up secure API connections to LLM providers (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic)
Draft and benchmark prompt templates for structured marketing copy generation
Establish data flow diagrams for the 1-click orchestration pipeline

phases.title: Core Generation Engine & API Development
deliverables: Structured JSON parser for LLM outputs
Social media scheduling and email service integrations
tasks: Build backend services to process user campaign inputs and trigger LLM chains
Implement robust parsing logic to convert raw LLM text into structured JSON assets
Integrate third-party APIs for email delivery (e.g., SendGrid) and social scheduling (e.g., Buffer)
Develop unit tests for the generation engine to handle API rate limits and retries

phases.title: Frontend UI & 1-Click Orchestration
deliverables: Interactive content preview and editing panels
tasks: Develop the frontend interface for inputting campaign goals, target audience, and dates
Build preview components allowing users to review and edit generated emails, landing pages, and social posts
Implement the frontend-to-backend state management for the 1-click launch execution
Conduct usability testing with the marketing team to refine the user experience

phases.title: Testing, Security & Production Deployment
deliverables: Security audit and API key management system
tasks: Perform comprehensive integration testing of the entire 1-click campaign launch flow
Conduct security reviews, ensuring encryption of sensitive API keys and user data
Optimize LLM latency, token usage, and implement caching strategies
Deploy the application to production and establish monitoring and alerting systems

phases.title: Data Ingestion & Source Integration
deliverables: Configured APIs for news screening and compliance updates
tasks: Establish secure connections to trading partner behavioral databases
Integrate external news and regulatory compliance RSS/API feeds
Design the centralized data schema for risk profiling

phases.title: AI Risk Scoring & NLP Model Development
tasks: Develop NLP models to scan news for negative sentiment and compliance flags
Build a weighted risk scoring engine based on historical behavior and news alerts
Train and validate models on historical partner default and risk datasets

phases.title: Real-Time Alerting & UI Integration
tasks: Develop the automated alerting mechanism for high-risk flags
Build frontend dashboard for compliance officers to view partner risk profiles
Connect the risk scoring engine outputs to the dashboard UI

phases.title: Validation, Testing & Production Deployment
tasks: Conduct end-to-end integration and security testing
Perform UAT with compliance and legal teams
Deploy the automated risk detection system to production
Establish monitoring and logging for model drift

phases.title: Data Integration & Predictive Modeling
tasks: Extract historical CRM interaction and transaction data
Perform exploratory data analysis and feature engineering
Train and evaluate machine learning models for customer churn and engagement risk

phases.title: Dynamic Segmentation Engine Development
tasks: Develop data ingestion pipelines for live user activity
Build the dynamic scoring engine to assign users to behavioral segments
Expose secure API endpoints for CRM platform queries

phases.title: Campaign Automation & Offer Mapping
tasks: Map predictive segments to specific retention offers
Configure automated triggers within the CRM platform
Set up control groups and A/B test variants for campaign performance tracking

phases.title: Deployment, Monitoring & Optimization
tasks: Execute end-to-end integration and user acceptance testing
Deploy the segmentation engine to production infrastructure
Build dashboards to monitor open-rates, click-through rates, and retention metrics

phases.title: Data Ingestion & Pipeline Setup
deliverables: ETL pipelines for historical partner metrics (registrations, deposits, volume)
tasks: Map data sources across existing dashboards and databases
Configure secure data ingestion pipelines for real-time and batch processing
Design database schema optimized for time-series partner activity
Establish data access controls and encryption protocols

phases.title: AI Model Development & Anomaly Detection
tasks: Perform feature engineering on partner activity and conversion metrics
Train machine learning models for trend analysis and anomaly detection
Validate model accuracy against historical partner churn data
Develop rule-based recommendation logic for proactive partner management

phases.title: Alerting Engine & Dashboard Integration
tasks: Develop frontend dashboard for partner health visualization and risk scoring
Build backend alert generation service based on model outputs
Integrate recommended actions into the partner manager UI
Configure notification channels and threshold settings for custom alerts

phases.title: Testing, Deployment & Optimization
tasks: Conduct User Acceptance Testing (UAT) with Partner Managers
Perform end-to-end security audits and system load testing
Deploy application to production environment
